results.title: India Cements Ltd. vs. Commissioner Of Income Tax, Madras.
results.snippet: 17. The learned counsel relied upon the decisions reported in 298 ITR 194 (SC) (Deputy Commissioner of Income Tax v. Core Health Care Ltd.,), 299 ITR 85 (SC) (Deputy Commissioner of Income Tax v. Gujarat Alkalies and Chemicals Ltd.), 205 CTR (Mad) 498(Commissioner of Income Tax v. Carborandum Universal Ltd.), 220 ITR 185 (Veecumsees v. Commissioner of Income Tax ), 60 ITR 52 (India Cements Ltd. Vs. Commissioner of Income Tax ), 293 ITR 459 (Commissioner Of Income - Tax v. Rane (Madras) Ltd.), 175 ITR 212 (Commissioner of Income Tax Vs. Hindustan Machine Tools Ltd.), 175 ITR 215 (Commissioner of Income Tax v. Indian Telephone Industries Ltd.),293 ITR 231(Commissioner of Income Tax v. Relaxo Footwears Ltd.), 311 ITR 405 (Jay Engineering Works Ltd. v. Commissioner of Income Tax ), 164 ITR 525 (Commissioner Of Income - Tax v. S.S.M Ahmed Hussain), 177 ITR 377 (Alembic Chemical Works Co. Ltd. v. Commissioner of Income Tax ), 150 CTR 126 (Commissioner of Income Tax v. I.A.E.C(Pumps) Ltd.) : 232 ITR 316, and 290 ITR 196(Commissioner Of Income - Tax v. Thirani Chemicals Ltd.) in support of his submissions. The learned counsel also submitted that the decision reported in 315 ITR 114 (Commissioner of Income Tax v. Sri Mangayarkarasi Mills P. Ltd.), 322 ITR 20 (Assistant Commissioner of Income Tax v. Elecon Engineering Co. Ltd.) and 312 ITR 254 (Commissioner of Income Tax v. Woodward Governor India P. Ltd.) were all distinguishable and the same are not applicable to the facts of this case.

results.title: Sir Shadi Lal And Sons vs. Commissioner Of Income Tax
results.snippet: 18. The decision of this Court in Sir Shadi Lal and sons v. Commissioner of Income Tax (supra) has been impliedly approved by Hon'ble the Supreme Court in Commissioner Of Income Tax v. Sun Engineering Works (P) Ltd. The Chettinad Corporation P. Ltd. v. Commissioner of Income Tax (supra) has also been impliedly approved by Hon'ble the Supreme Court in Sun Engineering case.19. The legal position regarding the present issue has been set at rest by Hon'ble the Supreme Court in Commissioner Of Income Tax v. Sun Engineering Works (P) Ltd. (supra), which is as under:- 9. Learned counsel for the assessee has relied upon Income Tax Officer v. K.L Srihari (HUF) (2001) 250 ITR 193, Commissioner of Income Tax v. Mysore Iron and Steel Limited (1986) 157 ITR 531, Commissioner of Income Tax v. Mahalaxmi Sugar Mills Co. Ltd. (1986) 160 ITR 920 (SC), Additional Commissioner of Income Tax v. Sheetalaya (1979) 117 ITR 658, Commissioner of Income Tax v. Shree Bajrang Electric Steel Co.(P) Ltd. (1988) 174 ITR 672, State Bank of Hyderabad v. Commissioner of Income Tax (1988) 171 ITR 232 (Andhra Pradesh High Court), Sir Shadi Lal and sons v. Commissioner of Income Tax (1973) 92 ITR 453, Additional Commissioner of Income Tax v. Gurjargravures P. Ltd. (1978) 111 ITR 1 (SC), V. Jaganmohan Rao v. Commissioner of Income Tax (1970) 75 ITR 373, Commissioner Of Income Tax v. Sun Engineering Works (P) Ltd. (1992) 198 ITR 297, Income Tax Officer v. K.L Srihari (1992) 197 ITR 694, Smt. Raj Rani Gulati v. Commissioner of Income Tax ( Income Tax Appeal No. 54 of 2007 decided by Lucknow Bench on 19.10.2011 and Commissioner Of Income Tax , Bangalore v. J.H Gotla, Yadagiri. (1985) 156 ITR 323 (SC).10. Sri Shambhu Chopra appearing for revenue has relied upon Commissioner Of Income Tax v. Sun Engineering Works (P) Ltd. (1992) 198 ITR 297 and Chettinad Corporation P. Ltd. v. Commissioner of Income Tax (1993) 200 ITR 320.
